summary: Regression + new-feature testing after dark-mode CSS refactor and review-delete feature addition. Backend: extended backend_test.py with 7 new tests for admin review delete/recalculation + admin/reviews list; full suite now 38/38 passing. Frontend: verified dark<->light theme toggle switches entire app (Landing, Services, Admin/Customer/Provider dashboards, Chat) with good contrast in both themes, persists across navigation, Admin Reviews tab lists reviews with avatar/Verified Customer badge/rating/date/photos, delete review works end-to-end (toast confirms, count decrements, service rating_avg/rating_count recalculates to 0/0 when last review deleted per backend test), provider dashboard has NO review delete capability (confirmed), invalid login stays on /login, Services page filters/cards render correctly, AI chat assistant streams/returns responses correctly, mobile menu (390x844) opens correctly.

ui_bugs.issue: Earnings value renders raw unrounded float e.g. '$420.2000000000' which overflows/gets clipped in the card instead of formatting to 2 decimal places (e.g. $420.20)
ui_bugs.selector: provider dashboard Overview tab, Total Earnings stat card

ui_bugs.issue: Dark/light theme toggle button is completely absent from the mobile hamburger menu (only present in the 'hidden md:flex' desktop nav section) — mobile users have no way to switch themes at all. Review request explicitly asked to verify toggle on mobile (390x844).

action_items: Add theme toggle button inside the mobile menu dropdown in Navbar.jsx so mobile users can switch dark/light mode (currently only visible in desktop nav via 'hidden md:flex').
Format provider dashboard Total Earnings (and any other currency stat) with toFixed(2) or similar to avoid raw floating point display / card overflow.
critical_code_review_comments: reviews.py delete_review and recalculate_rating logic is correct and matches spec: verified via both curl (main agent) and pytest (this session) that deleting last review resets rating_avg/rating_count to 0.
admin.py GET /admin/reviews correctly joins service_title and excludes _id via review dict conversion - no ObjectId leakage observed.

AdminReviews.jsx and ServiceDetail.jsx both use semantic tokens (bg-card, border-border, text-foreground, text-muted-foreground) correctly - dark mode refactor did not introduce contrast issues on reviewed pages.
Provider dashboard correctly has no review-delete affordance anywhere (verified no admin-review-delete-button-* elements present).

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (2 minor UI bugs found, all functional flows working)

test_credentials: admin@quickfix.com/Admin@123, marcus.plumbing@quickfix.com/Password@123, jordan.customer@quickfix.com/Password@123 - all verified working per /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: backend_test.py fixture review_service_and_booking creates TEST_ReviewSvc_* service + booking + review for delete-recalc tests; test data is consumed/deleted by the tests themselves (review deleted twice to hit 404 case, service soft-deleted at end of run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully passing (38/38, including new review-delete/admin-reviews coverage). Dark mode toggle verified working correctly across Landing/Services/Admin/Provider/Customer/Chat pages on desktop (1920x1080) with good contrast in both themes. Two frontend bugs found and NOT yet fixed: (1) mobile menu missing theme toggle entirely, (2) provider dashboard earnings stat shows unrounded float overflowing card. Both are small, isolated fixes. Next agent should re-verify these two fixes visually (screenshot mobile menu + provider dashboard) once addressed; no other regression concerns found.
rca_of_the_issue: Mobile theme toggle bug: Navbar.jsx wraps the theme-toggle <button> inside a div with className 'hidden items-center gap-3 md:flex' (desktop-only), and the separate mobile dropdown block (open && ... md:hidden) only renders nav links (Browse Services, Dashboard, Logout/Login) without ever including the theme toggle button, so it was simply omitted during the CSS refactor. Earnings float bug is likely `earnings.toFixed()` missing or raw provider_stats.earnings sum from MongoDB aggregation being rendered directly via ${earnings} in StatCard without formatting.
